title: Support pi ChatGPT Codex subscription auth in Vellum stream proxy
description:
## Problem

Vellum's browser chat currently proxies LLM calls through the Express route `POST /api/stream` (`src/server/lib/stream-route.ts`). That route only resolves provider credentials from environment variables or API-key files:

- `VELLUM_LLM_API_KEY`
- `${PROVIDER}_API_KEY`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
- `OPENAI_API_KEY`
- `VELLUM_LLM_API_KEY_FILE`
- `${PROVIDER}_API_KEY_FILE`
- `API_KEY_FILE`

This means users who only have a **ChatGPT Plus/Pro Coding/Codex subscription authenticated through pi-agent** cannot use Vellum's browser chat, even though pi itself supports that subscription via `/login` and stores OAuth credentials in `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`.

This is a launch blocker: the app must work with the same ChatGPT Coding plan credentials that pi-agent already uses.

## Current relevant state

- Browser entry point: `src/main.ts`
  - Default model is currently Anthropic Sonnet:
    - `getModel("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514")`
  - Browser uses `streamProxy` to send requests to same-origin `/api/stream`.
- Server stream route: `src/server/lib/stream-route.ts`
  - Parses `{ model, context, options }`.
  - Calls `resolveApiKey(parsed.request.model.provider)`.
  - Passes returned string as `apiKey` to `streamSimple`.
- pi-agent docs say subscription auth is stored in `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`.
- pi-ai has built-in OpenAI Codex support:
  - Provider: `openai-codex`
  - API: `openai-codex-responses`
  - Models include `gpt-5.1`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, etc.
  - OAuth helper exists at `@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/utils/oauth/openai-codex.js` and exports `refreshOpenAICodexToken` / `openaiCodexOAuthProvider`.
  - The provider extracts `chatgpt_account_id` from the access token, so `streamSimple` only needs a valid refreshed access token as `apiKey`.

## Goal

Allow Vellum's browser app to use a ChatGPT Plus/Pro Coding/Codex subscription that was already authenticated via pi-agent (`pi` `/login` → ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Codex), without requiring an OpenAI Platform API key.

## Required behavior

1. If the browser requests model provider `openai-codex`, `/api/stream` must resolve credentials from pi-agent's `auth.json` when env/API-key credentials are absent.
2. If the stored OAuth access token is expired or near expiry, refresh it with `refreshOpenAICodexToken(refresh)` before calling `streamSimple`.
3. Persist refreshed OAuth credentials back to `auth.json` with mode `0600`, preserving other provider entries.
4. Keep existing env/file API-key behavior intact and with higher priority than `auth.json`.
5. Return clear, actionable errors when credentials are missing or malformed, e.g.:
   - `No API key or pi OAuth credentials configured for openai-codex. Run pi, /login, and choose ChatGPT Plus/Pro (Codex).`
6. Prefer a ChatGPT Codex default model in the browser when usable, or expose a simple, documented path for selecting it in the ChatPanel model selector.
   - Preferred default for this bead: use `getModel("openai-codex", "gpt-5.1-codex-mini")` or another available `openai-codex` model from `getModels("openai-codex")`.
   - If keeping Anthropic as default, add explicit UI/config logic so users with only ChatGPT Codex can select/use an `openai-codex` model without editing source.

## Implementation guidance

### New auth helper module

Create a small server-only helper, suggested path:

`src/server/lib/pi-auth.ts`

Responsibilities:

- Locate pi auth file:
  - Default: `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`
  - Respect `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` if set, matching pi's config behavior (`$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/auth.json`).
  - Optional test override env var is OK, e.g. `VELLUM_PI_AUTH_FILE`.
- Parse auth JSON safely.
- Support at least this shape for OAuth entries:

```json
{
  "openai-codex": {
    "type": "oauth",
    "access": "...jwt...",
    "refresh": "...",
    "expires": 1770000000000,
    "accountId": "..."
  }
}
```

- Be tolerant if `type` is absent but `access`, `refresh`, and `expires` are present, because older pi migrations may have written OAuth objects with slightly different shape.
- Validate field types before use.
- Refresh when `expires <= Date.now() + 60_000`.
- Use dynamic import for OAuth helpers if needed to avoid browser/Vite bundling issues:

```ts
const { refreshOpenAICodexToken } = await import("@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/utils/oauth/openai-codex.js");
```

This module must only be imported by server code.

### Stream route changes

Update `src/server/lib/stream-route.ts` so credential resolution can be async and provider-aware:

- Current type:

```ts
resolveApiKey?: (provider: string) => string | undefined;
```

- Suggested type:

```ts
resolveApiKey?: (provider: string) => Promise<string | undefined> | string | undefined;
```

- Default resolver should try:
  1. Existing env/file logic.
  2. `openai-codex` OAuth from pi auth file.
  3. Future-proof extension point for other pi OAuth providers is OK, but not required.

Do not break existing tests that stub `resolveApiKey` synchronously.

### Browser default/model selection

Update `src/main.ts` as needed.

Acceptance preference:

- Default model is `openai-codex` when the app is intended to run for a user with only ChatGPT Coding plan.
- If there is no reliable browser-side way to know server credentials, using `openai-codex` as the default is acceptable for this project because this credential path is required.
- Use `getModel("openai-codex", <known model id>)`; verify the ID exists with `getModels("openai-codex")`.

## Tests

Add unit tests. Suggested files:

- `src/server/lib/pi-auth.test.ts`
- Extend `src/server/lib/stream-route.test.ts`
- Update `src/main.test.ts` if the default model changes

Required test cases:

1. Resolves existing env key for `openai-codex` before reading pi auth.
2. Reads valid `openai-codex` OAuth credentials from a temp auth file and returns the `access` token.
3. Refreshes expired `openai-codex` credentials using a mocked `refreshOpenAICodexToken`, returns the new access token, and writes refreshed credentials back to disk without deleting other providers.
4. Does not refresh when token expires more than 60 seconds in the future.
5. Returns `undefined` for missing auth file, missing provider entry, malformed JSON, or malformed credential entry.
6. `createStreamRoute()` awaits async resolver results and passes the resolved token to `streamSimple`.
7. Missing credentials for `openai-codex` produce an actionable error message.
8. Existing Anthropic/OpenAI env-key tests still pass.
9. Typecheck and full test suite pass.

For tests, do not read or mutate the real `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`. Use a temp file and `VELLUM_PI_AUTH_FILE` or inject paths/options into the helper.

## Manual verification

After implementation:

1. Authenticate pi with ChatGPT Codex if not already done:

```text
pi
/login
# choose ChatGPT Plus/Pro (Codex)
```

2. Confirm `~/.pi/agent/auth.json` contains an `openai-codex` OAuth entry.
3. Start Vellum:

```bash
nix develop --command bash -c 'npm run server'
# second terminal
nix develop --command bash -c 'npm run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1'
```

4. Open `http://127.0.0.1:5173`.
5. Send a simple prompt and verify an assistant response streams without `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set.
6. Send a tool prompt:

```text
Find playable positions for F4 on baroque-lute-13.
```

7. Verify the agent can call tools and continue the conversation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Vellum browser chat works with only pi-agent ChatGPT Plus/Pro Codex OAuth credentials and no OpenAI Platform key.
- [ ] `/api/stream` supports provider `openai-codex` via `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`.
- [ ] Expired Codex OAuth tokens are refreshed and persisted.
- [ ] Existing env/file API-key behavior remains unchanged and takes precedence.
- [ ] Browser default/model selection supports `openai-codex` out of the box.
- [ ] Error messages tell users to run `pi` → `/login` → ChatGPT Plus/Pro (Codex) when missing credentials.
- [ ] No browser bundle imports server-only Node modules or OAuth helper modules.
- [ ] `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npx vite build` all pass in `nix develop`.

## Non-goals

- Do not implement a browser OAuth login flow in Vellum.
- Do not store subscription tokens in browser localStorage/sessionStorage.
- Do not require an OpenAI Platform API key for this path.
- Do not remove existing Anthropic/OpenAI API-key support.
notes: Implementation complete and automated quality gates pass in nix develop (npm run typecheck, npm test, npx vite build). Awaiting user browser verification with real pi ChatGPT Codex OAuth credentials.
close_reason: Implemented pi ChatGPT Codex OAuth auth, fixed browser ChatPanel storage/CSS wiring, switched default to supported gpt-5.3-codex, and verified in browser with a streamed response.

title: Use artifacts panel for large compile previews
description:
## Problem

Compile results currently render full SVG notation inline inside the chat transcript. For real scores this appears as a tiny preview in a chat bubble while the large right-hand artifacts/preview area remains unused or underused. This makes rendered notation hard to inspect and makes the chat feel cramped.

## Desired behavior

- Chat remains conversation/status/tool-log focused.
- Successful `compile` SVG/PDF output is promoted into the right-hand preview/artifacts panel.
- The compile tool message in chat shows a compact summary such as "Compiled successfully — preview opened" plus errors if any.
- The preview should use the available right-side space, support scrolling/zoom or fit-to-width, and remain usable for multi-page/large SVG output.
- Error output should continue to render clearly in chat.

## Acceptance criteria

- A successful compile opens or updates the right-side preview panel with the rendered score/tab SVG.
- Chat does not embed a large/tiny score preview by default.
- Users can inspect the full render in the larger panel.
- Existing compile error rendering remains visible in chat.
- Browser smoke test covers compile -> preview panel behavior.

## Notes

Related observation from manual testing: the current inline compile renderer makes the score appear in a tiny window in the chat instead of using the big open space to the right.
close_reason: Implemented compile preview promotion to the artifacts panel with compact chat summary, zoom/fit controls, and browser layout smoke verification.

title: Enforce compile-after-generation and bounded compile retry loop
description:
## Problem

The browser agent currently treats LilyPond rendering as optional and does not consistently recover from compilation failures:

1. After generating or modifying LilyPond source, the assistant may stop and present the source without immediately calling `compile`, leaving users without the expected SVG preview.
2. When `compile` fails, the assistant may ask the user for permission to continue instead of autonomously fixing the source and recompiling.
3. Generated LilyPond often fails immediately because the source relies on automatic pitch-to-string placement for historical/re-entrant tunings where LilyPond cannot infer playable strings/frets. The assistant should either produce validated/playable source or admit failure after bounded retries.

Observed failure examples:

```text
3 error(s):
Line 25: No string for pitch #<Pitch g, > (given frets ())
Line 26: No string for pitch #<Pitch d, > (given frets ())
Line 26: No string for pitch #<Pitch d, > (given frets ())
```

And assistant behavior such as:

```text
I can fix this by writing a tab-first version with explicit, playable course/fret mapping ..., then compile again.

Proceed and I’ll return only when it renders successfully.
```

The assistant should not ask the user to proceed after it has already promised to render; it should iterate automatically.

## Desired behavior

- Any assistant turn that generates or modifies LilyPond source must immediately render it by calling `compile` with SVG output before presenting the work as complete.
- If `compile` returns errors, the agent must diagnose the errors, revise the LilyPond source, and call `compile` again without asking the user for permission.
- Retry should be bounded, e.g. max 3 compile attempts per generated source request, to avoid infinite loops.
- If all retries fail, the assistant should clearly admit it could not generate compilable LilyPond, summarize the final errors, and avoid claiming success.
- The assistant should prefer tab-first/validated generation for historical plucked instruments: use `tabulate`, `voicings`, `check_playability`, and instrument profile constraints to avoid unreachable pitches and LilyPond auto-string errors.
- Successful compile should continue to open/update the right-side preview panel via the existing compile preview behavior.

## Implementation options

Use one or more of these; choose the most robust approach during implementation:

1. **Prompt policy:** strengthen `src/prompts.ts` so the system prompt explicitly says:
   - Do not stop after emitting LilyPond source; immediately call `compile`.
   - On compile failure, do not ask the user whether to continue; fix and recompile up to N times.
   - Only report success after an SVG/PDF artifact is returned.

2. **Agent/runtime guard:** add browser-side agent steering in `src/main.ts` or a helper module:
   - Detect failed `compile` tool results.
   - Inject a follow-up instruction/message forcing the agent to continue fixing and recompiling.
   - Track per-request retry count and stop after the configured limit with a final failure explanation.
   - Ensure this guard does not trigger on successful compile or compile tool transport errors that require user/server intervention.

3. **Tool affordance / combined workflow:** add or adjust a tool so the preferred model workflow is hard to misuse, e.g.:
   - a `render_lilypond`/`compile_preview` tool alias with a description that says it is mandatory after any LilyPond generation, or
   - a higher-level validated LilyPond/tab generation tool that accepts structured playable positions and then compiles.

4. **Backend/tool diagnostics:** improve compile failure details where possible:
   - preserve LilyPond errors in structured `details.errors`,
   - include line snippets/context if available,
   - classify common errors such as `No string for pitch ...` as likely unreachable/ambiguous string assignment,
   - suggest using tab-first explicit string/fret mapping or `tabulate`/instrument validation.

A pure backend-only auto-fix is not required for arbitrary LilyPond because the backend cannot infer musical intent, but deterministic validation/diagnostics are encouraged.

## Acceptance criteria

- When the user asks for an arrangement/rendering and the assistant generates LilyPond, the agent calls `compile` in the same workflow before claiming completion.
- A failed compile automatically triggers at least one revise-and-recompile attempt without user intervention.
- The agent stops after a documented bounded retry limit, reports failure honestly, and includes the final compile errors.
- The assistant no longer says variants of “Proceed and I’ll compile/fix it” after a compile failure for the same request.
- Successful final compile opens/updates the artifacts preview panel and chat shows the compact compile success summary.
- Existing compile error rendering remains visible in chat during failed attempts.
- Unit tests cover the prompt policy and any retry/steering helper logic.
- Browser smoke test covers a mocked or controlled compile-failure-then-success path and verifies the preview panel opens only after success.
- `npm run typecheck`, targeted tests, and `npm run build` pass.

## Test suggestions

- Prompt test: assert system prompt contains mandatory compile-after-LilyPond and bounded retry instructions.
- Retry guard test: simulate `tool_execution_end` for `compile` with errors and verify a follow-up/steering instruction is queued exactly until retry limit.
- Retry guard test: simulate successful compile and verify no retry instruction is queued.
- Browser smoke: mock or trigger compile failure followed by corrected source; verify the right preview panel contains SVG after success and no large SVG is embedded in chat.

## Non-goals

- Do not implement an unbounded self-repair loop.
- Do not silently claim success when compilation fails.
- Do not require the user to approve routine compile-failure fixes within the same requested render task.
- Do not move LLM/API credentials into browser code.
close_reason: Implemented mandatory compile prompt policy, browser retry steering guard with bounded retry limit, string-assignment diagnostics, tests, and browser smoke verification.

title: Fix: parsePitch accepts x (double-sharp) but accidentalToLy doesn't convert it
description:
## What
Fix the handling of `x` (double-sharp symbol) in the pitch conversion pipeline in `src/lib/pitch.ts`.

## Why
`parsePitch()` accepts `x` in its accidental regex pattern `([#bx♯♭]*)`, and normalizes `♯`→`#` and `♭`→`b`, but leaves `x` untouched. Then `accidentalToLy()` only handles `#`→`is` and `b`→`es`, so `x` passes through as-is.

Result: `scientificToLilyPond("Cx4")` → `"cx'cisis\##x##parsePitch()♯#♭btypescript
accidental: accidental.replaceAll(♯, #).replaceAll(♭, b).replaceAll(x, ##),
accidentalToLy#isscientificToLilyPond(Cx4)cisis\"`
- `parsePitch("Cx4")` returns `{ letter: "C", accidental: "##", octave: 4 }`
- No regression on existing pitch tests
close_reason: done

title: Epic: Generic music import and arrangement workflow
description:
What: Implement HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md as a generic import-normalize-arrange pipeline. Vellum should accept supported pasted score/text sources, normalize them into a canonical MusicDocument IR, infer source profile(s), preserve the material that defines the profile, and generate idiomatic historical plucked-instrument arrangements.

Why: The Be Thou My Vision trace showed Vellum can produce good results only after the user clarifies that hymn material must preserve melody, lyrics, repeats, and harmony. That workflow should be the default for hymn/chorale sources while the architecture remains generic for lead sheets, melody-only tunes, part music, and later artifacts.

How: Keep import profile-agnostic and arrangement profile-aware. Add source detection, MusicDocument schemas, server-side music_import, MusicXML import, restricted LilyPond import, normalization/analysis, preservation-target selection, multi-staff engrave support, harmony reduction, and alfabeto planning. Use MusicXML as the preferred interchange. Treat PDF/image/.mscz/MIDI as post-v1 artifact paths unless converted to supported text first.

Considerations: Do not create hymn_import or a SATB-only schema. Do not claim full LilyPond interpretation. Do not silently drop unsupported constructs. Do not make arrange_music an opaque black box; expose deterministic stages and diagnostics. If multi-staff/repeat engrave support is absent, the shipped MVP must explicitly limit itself to melody + simple tab.

Acceptance criteria: V1 accepts pasted MusicXML and documented restricted LilyPond, returns a full MusicDocument inline from music_import, infers hymn/chorale, SATB-like, lead-sheet, and melody-only profiles, preserves lead material by default, emits diagnostics for unsupported/ambiguous input, and can produce a historical plucked-instrument arrangement that compiles for the supported engrave feature set.

comments.author: jeff
comments.text: Hardened HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md per blunder hunt: narrowed v1 to text-first import, added restricted LilyPond parser scope, separated MusicXML import from analyze, expanded IR for SATB/onsets/lyrics/provenance, split artifact import into later path, clarified engrave prerequisites and transposition policy. Implementation remains open.

title: Define canonical MusicDocument IR, schemas, and rational duration utilities
description:
What: Define TypeScript/TypeBox schemas and runtime types for the canonical MusicDocument IR from HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md, plus rational duration utilities used by importers, normalizers, validators, and engrave.

Why: Every supported source format must normalize into one source-format-neutral representation so arrangement logic does not depend on LilyPond syntax, MusicXML traversal details, or SATB-specific assumptions.

How: Model global measures, source artifacts, metadata, profile candidates, musical structure sections/repeats, parts, voices, events with measureId/onset/duration, lyric stanzas and event anchors, harmony analysis, normalized analysis, source locations, diagnostics, and provenance/license fields. Durations are rational quarter-note units with source-token preservation.

Considerations: SATB/four-part writing must be represented as ordinary parts/voices plus inferred roles, not a special top-level shape. Lyrics attach to stable event IDs. All dropped/rewritten/unsupported constructs must have diagnostics. Avoid persisting imports in v1; return inline MusicDocument values.

Acceptance criteria: Schemas compile, are exported for tools/tests, cover every IR type in the spec, include validation helpers for rational durations/source diagnostics, and have unit tests for representative MusicXML, LilyPond, lead-sheet, melody-only, SATB, lyrics-only, and unknown-profile documents.

title: Music import: implement source-format and profile detection
description:
What: Implement SourceDetection for pasted text sources, returning ranked source-format candidates and ranked profile candidates with evidence and diagnostics.

Why: The agent should not reason musically from raw syntax or ask users to classify sources first. Detection is the gateway that selects MusicXML/LilyPond/ABC/MEI/plain-text importers and sets expectations for preservation targets.

How: Detect LilyPond tokens (version, score, relative, Staff, lyricmode, addlyrics), MusicXML roots (score-partwise/timewise), ABC headers (X/T/K/M), MEI roots, and plain lyrics signals. Infer profiles including hymn, chorale, lead_sheet, monophonic_tune, part_song, instrumental_score, tablature, plain_lyrics, and unknown. Return selectedFormat/selectedProfiles only when confidence is high enough.

Considerations: Do not classify ABC as plain lyrics if ABC headers are present. Binary artifact detection by filename/MIME belongs to the later artifact path, not v1 pasted text. Ambiguity should produce diagnostics and candidates, not silent guesses.

Acceptance criteria: Unit tests cover LilyPond, MusicXML, ABC, MEI, plain lyrics, hymn/chorale, lead-sheet, melody-only, multi-part, and ambiguous/unknown fixtures with stable confidence/evidence output.

title: Music import: define complete TypeBox schemas for MusicDocument and related IR
description:
What: Create TypeBox schemas for MusicDocument, SourceArtifactRef, MusicMetadata, MusicalStructure/Section, ImportedMeasure, MusicPart, MusicVoice, ImportedEvent variants, lyrics, HarmonyAnalysis, NormalizedAnalysis, SourceDetection, and SourceDiagnostic.

Why: Tool inputs/outputs, tests, normalizers, and engrave builders need one validated contract. A schema-first IR prevents each importer from inventing incompatible object shapes.

How: Mirror the spec's TypeScript model while adapting names to project conventions. Export schemas and inferred types from a shared library module. Ensure events carry stable IDs, measureId, onset, duration, and optional sourceLocation. Preserve snake_case only at external tool boundaries; internal types should remain idiomatic and consistent.

Considerations: Do not use HymnDocument naming; hymn/chorale is a profile, not the IR. Do not collapse simultaneous voices or SATB into a special schema. Preserve optional provenance/license fields for user-facing rights warnings without adjudicating rights.

Acceptance criteria: Schema validation accepts representative valid documents and rejects missing IDs, invalid roles, invalid profile names, malformed durations, unanchored required measure references, and unsupported event variants.

title: Music import: implement rational duration math and bar-duration validation helpers
description:
What: Implement rational quarter-note duration utilities for parsing, normalization, addition/subtraction, comparison, reduction, dotted durations, and measure-sum validation.

Why: Imported sources expose pickups, dotted rhythms, repeated sections, and voice-specific durations. Deterministic rational arithmetic is required before LilyPond codegen to avoid barcheck failures and compile retries.

How: Represent durations as numerator/denominator in quarter-note units with optional sourceToken. Provide helpers for time-signature expected measure duration, pickup handling, alternatives, explicit duration overrides, and per-voice/per-measure validation diagnostics.

Considerations: Avoid floating-point offsets. Preserve original tokens for diagnostics while exposing normalized rational values. Tuplets are unsupported in LilyPond v1 and should surface as diagnostics rather than approximate floats.

Acceptance criteria: Unit tests cover ordinary note values, dotted notes, ties, rests, pickup measures, 3/4 and common-time bars, alternative endings, mismatched voices, and diagnostic messages with measure IDs.
close_reason: Completed: added rational quarter-note duration helpers in src/lib/music-utils.ts, including reduction/arithmetic/comparison, LilyPond duration parsing with dotted values and tuplets rejected, time-signature expected durations, pickup/override measure validation, and aggregate diagnostics. Tests cover ordinary values, dotted durations, written ties/rest durations via bar sums, pickups, 3/4/common-time, alternative-ending overrides, mismatched voices, and mixed inputs. Quality gates pass: npm run typecheck; npm test; npm run format:check.

comments.author: jeff
comments.text: Agent update: claimed after bv triage highlighted vellum-n9o.1 as the highest-impact blocker and vellum-n9o.1.3 as the ready child that directly unblocks multi-staff engrave schema work. I will implement rational quarter-note duration utilities and validation tests.

title: Music import: add shared source diagnostics, locations, provenance, and licensing helpers
description:
What: Implement helpers for SourceLocation, SourceDiagnostic, SourceArtifactRef, provenance/license metadata, and diagnostic aggregation used by all importers and planners.

Why: The spec requires every lossy transformation, unsupported construct, ambiguity, and rights/provenance datum to remain visible to the user and future arrangement stages.

How: Provide constructors for info/warning/error diagnostics with code, message, sourceId, line/column/measure where available. Add summary functions for tool responses and prompt-facing suggested next actions.

Considerations: Vellum should preserve and display rights/provenance but not auto-adjudicate copyright. Diagnostics are also the v1 uncertainty-review surface, replacing any richer side panel until post-v1.

Acceptance criteria: Importers can emit consistent diagnostics; music_import responses summarize them; tests assert unsupported constructs are reported with stable codes rather than being silently dropped.

title: Music import: implement deterministic normalization and analysis pass
description:
What: Run a deterministic analysis pass after parsing and before arrangement, filling NormalizedAnalysis with quantized offsets, texture classification, inferred lead, voice roles, key/range hints, phrases/cadences where feasible, and normalization diagnostics.

Why: Importers should only parse source material; arrangers need stable, source-format-neutral musical facts. This pass is where MusicXML and LilyPond imports converge.

How: Use music21 where helpful for MusicXML/MEI/MIDI-like streams and local analyzers where needed. Infer monophonic, melody_with_accompaniment, homophonic_four_part, homophonic_multi_part, polyphonic, lead_sheet, tablature, lyrics_only, or unknown textures with evidence.

Considerations: Do not assume SATB. SATB is a strong special case of labeled/register-ordered voices. Emit diagnostics for ambiguous voice splits, unquantized rhythms, unsupported tuplets, and incomplete lyric alignment.

Acceptance criteria: Normalization tests cover rational offsets, texture classification, lead candidates, SATB-like role inference, non-SATB role inference, key estimation, ambitus/range data, and diagnostic stability.

title: Music import support: improve existing tabulate, playability, compile, and alfabeto tool boundaries
description:
What: Implement supporting improvements identified by HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md: tabulate LilyPond pitch spelling hints, tabulate_many batch lookup, onset-aware check_playability, targeted compile repair hints, and alfabeto_lookup camelCase alias normalization.

Why: The trace burned tool calls on pitch/register discovery and LilyPond syntax repair. Better deterministic tools reduce retries and make the imported-arrangement pipeline more reliable.

How: Add batch APIs or aliases with tests. check_playability should distinguish sequential passages from simultaneous chords using onset times. compile should identify common note-duration-string order and barcheck failures. alfabeto_lookup should keep snake_case canonical but accept chordName/pitchClasses/chartId/maxFret/includeBarreVariants until callers migrate.

Considerations: Keep backward compatibility. Do not let convenience aliases obscure the canonical snake_case tool schema in prompts/docs.

Acceptance criteria: Tests cover both alfabeto naming styles, batch melody lookup, sequential vs simultaneous playability, and compile hints for `g\3 4` vs valid `g4\3`-style syntax plus barcheck failures.

title: Music import: build comprehensive unit, integration, and trace regression test suite
description:
What: Add a comprehensive test suite for source detection, MusicDocument schemas, importers, normalization, preservation planning, engrave conversion, alfabeto planning, and trace regressions.

Why: This workflow spans many formats and stages; regressions will otherwise appear as poor arrangements or compile retries far from the root cause.

How: Organize tests by stage with small fixtures and full pipeline integration cases. Include public-domain or generated fixture provenance. Run through npm test/typecheck/format gates.

Considerations: Some integration tests depend on multi-staff/repeat engrave support; mark or stage them according to dependencies instead of weakening assertions.

Acceptance criteria: Test coverage includes every v1 acceptance criterion in HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md, especially melody preservation, lyrics/repeats, non-SATB genericity, and valid LilyPond tab syntax.

title: Music import tests: add Be Thou My Vision trace regressions
description:
What: Add regression tests derived from the Be Thou My Vision debug trace that motivated the spec.

Why: The same failure mode must not recur: melody identity was initially dropped, lyrics/repeats were missed, and generated tab syntax needed repair.

How: Create fixtures for the initial melody-only phrase and the later old LilyPond SATB/five-verse/fivefold-repeat source. Assert the opening `G G A G | E D D E` identity remains in the lead, five stanzas and repeat structure import, and tab codegen uses valid LilyPond note-duration-string order.

Considerations: Keep fixtures legally safe and minimal. If exact source text has copyright ambiguity, use a synthetic trace-shaped fixture that exercises the same structures.

Acceptance criteria: Failing to preserve the lead phrase, losing repeats/lyrics, or emitting `g\3 4`-style syntax fails tests.

title: Music import tests: add end-to-end imported source to historical plucked arrangement cases
description:
What: Add end-to-end tests that import supported sources, normalize/analyze them, plan preservation/harmony/transposition, build engrave input, and compile supported historical plucked-instrument arrangements.

Why: Unit tests are not enough; the v1 Definition of Done requires the whole import-normalize-arrange-engrave-compile loop to work for happy-path sources.

How: Cover MusicXML hymn with lyrics/repeats, MusicXML lead sheet with chord symbols, melody-only restricted LilyPond, SATB restricted LilyPond after engrave support, non-SATB multi-part fixture, and baroque guitar alfabeto plan from imported harmony.

Considerations: If multi-staff/repeat engrave support is not complete, keep tests pending/blocked by the corresponding bead rather than pretending v1 is done.

Acceptance criteria: Supported e2e fixtures compile without raw LilyPond repairs and produce structured diagnostics/plan summaries that show preserved source material.

title: Implement music_import for pasted MusicXML text
description:
What: Implement the MusicXML import path for the server-side music_import tool. Pasted MusicXML should produce a full MusicDocument containing metadata, parts, measures, voices, lyrics, repeats/endings, chord symbols, directions, source locations, diagnostics, and profile evidence.

Why: MusicXML is the preferred v1 interchange for MuseScore, notation apps, scans converted by OMR, DAWs, and public-domain score sources. It is more stable than general LilyPond parsing and should be the most complete import path.

How: Build a dedicated importer rather than reusing the existing analyze summary as the data model. Use music21 or direct XML traversal where appropriate, but always emit the shared IR. Preserve part names, lyric verses, repeat/alternative structures, chord symbols, directions, key/time/tempo metadata, and confidence/diagnostic information.

Considerations: Existing analyze remains useful for harmonic analysis support, not authoritative import. Handle score-partwise/timewise roots explicitly. Unsupported MusicXML constructs should warn instead of being silently discarded.

Acceptance criteria: music_import(source_format=musicxml or auto) imports representative hymn/chorale, lead-sheet, melody-only, and non-SATB multi-part MusicXML fixtures into valid MusicDocument values with diagnostics and no source-format-specific leakage into downstream arrangers.

title: MusicXML import: parse metadata, measures, parts, voices, key/time/tempo
description:
What: Implement core MusicXML parsing for score metadata, part-list names, measures, voices/layers, key signatures, time signatures, tempo/directions where applicable, notes, rests, chords, ties, and normalized onsets/durations.

Why: These structures are the foundation for profile inference and arrangement. Without stable measures/voices/onsets the downstream pipeline reverts to lossy summaries.

How: Use music21 or direct XML traversal to build MusicDocument parts/voices/events with source locations and rational quarter durations. Preserve display measure numbers and original key/time metadata.

Considerations: Handle both score-partwise and score-timewise if feasible; otherwise emit clear diagnostics. Do not assume every score is SATB or has exactly one staff per part.

Acceptance criteria: Fixtures with simple melody, SATB hymn, lead sheet, and non-SATB multi-part score import notes/rests/chords into correct measures/voices with stable event IDs and valid durations.

title: MusicXML import: preserve lyrics, repeats, alternatives, chord symbols, and directions
description:
What: Extend MusicXML import to extract lyric verses/syllables, lyric anchors, repeat barlines, endings/alternatives, chord symbols/harmony elements, text directions, and related diagnostics.

Why: Hymnary and lead-sheet workflows depend on exactly the data that a pitch/rhythm-only importer would lose: words, repeat form, and chord/harmony cues.

How: Map MusicXML lyric elements to LyricStanza/LyricSyllable with event IDs; map repeat/endings into MusicalStructure sections; map harmony/chord-symbol elements to chord_symbol events and initial HarmonyAnalysis chord spans; preserve directions as events or measure-attached annotations.

Considerations: Lyric alignment may be partial; preserve unaligned stanza text and warn rather than inventing anchors. Repeat structures must reference existing measure IDs.

Acceptance criteria: Tests cover multi-verse lyrics, hyphenation/extenders, volta/ending structures, D.C./text directions where supported/diagnosed, chord symbols with bass notes, and incomplete lyric alignment warnings.

title: MusicXML import: add fixture suite for hymn, lead-sheet, melody-only, and non-SATB profiles
description:
What: Add public-domain or synthetic MusicXML fixtures and expected MusicDocument assertions for the main v1 source profiles.

Why: The importer must prove it is generic and not hard-coded to SATB hymns, even though hymn/chorale is a high-priority happy path.

How: Include at least one hymn/chorale with lyrics/repeats, one lead sheet with chord symbols, one melody-only tune, and one non-SATB multi-part score. Assert metadata, parts/roles, measures, events, lyrics, repeats, chord symbols, diagnostics, and profile candidates.

Considerations: Keep fixtures small enough for fast tests and clear review. Use public-domain or generated data with provenance notes.

Acceptance criteria: Fixtures run in npm test, fail clearly on IR regressions, and cover both source_format=musicxml and source_format=auto detection.

title: Implement documented restricted LilyPond text importer
description:
What: Implement the v1 restricted LilyPond importer for pasted hymnary-style and simple notation sources, producing the same MusicDocument IR as MusicXML import.

Why: Many hymnary and Mutopia-like sources are LilyPond text, and the motivating trace included old LilyPond containing SATB, lyrics, and repeats. Vellum should import the supported subset without pretending to be a full LilyPond interpreter.

How: Use python-ly where helpful for lexing/tree structure. Support header string fields, key/time/tempo, top-level music/lyric variables, simple Staff/Voice constructs, comments near voices, absolute and deterministic relative pitches, durations 1-64 with dots/ties/rests/chords/bar checks, simple simultaneous voices, repeat volta, alternatives, addlyrics, and lyricmode.

Considerations: Explicitly reject or warn on Scheme, includes, transposition macros, custom music functions, polymeter, tuplets, grace notes, figured bass, and content-changing macros without literal bodies. GPL dependency is acceptable for this project.

Acceptance criteria: Supported fixtures import cleanly; unsupported constructs generate SourceDiagnostic entries with source locations when possible; the trace-shaped old LilyPond fixture imports title/key/time/SATB-like voices/five lyric stanzas/fivefold repeat; no tests claim general LilyPond support.

title: LilyPond import: integrate python-ly and strict subset guardrails
description:
What: Add python-ly integration where useful for LilyPond lexing/tokenization/tree assistance and implement strict subset capability checks.

Why: Restricted LilyPond import benefits from existing tooling, but Vellum must not confuse tokenization help with full LilyPond interpretation.

How: Add the dependency to the server/runtime environment, wrap calls behind a project-local module, detect unsupported constructs early, and convert python-ly positions/tokens into SourceLocation and diagnostics.

Considerations: GPL is acceptable for this personal project. Includes, Scheme, custom functions, content-changing macros, and other unsupported constructs should produce deterministic diagnostics.

Acceptance criteria: Environment setup works under nix/npm test; unsupported-construct tests pass; importer can run without leaking python-specific errors to the user.

title: LilyPond import: parse headers, variables, staves, voices, and labels/comments
description:
What: Implement parsing for header string fields, simple top-level music/lyric variables, Staff/Voice/context constructs, and comments or identifiers that imply voice labels such as Soprano, Alto, Tenor, Bass, Melody, or Lead.

Why: Hymnary sources often encode essential metadata and voice roles in simple LilyPond variables and comments. The preservation planner needs those labels to choose the authoritative lead line.

How: Walk the restricted LilyPond structure, extract metadata into MusicMetadata, create MusicPart/MusicVoice entries, preserve labels/comments as evidence, and emit diagnostics for ignored layout/paper/engraving overrides.

Considerations: Do not execute arbitrary macros. Variables must have literal bodies in the pasted source. Layout preservation is out of scope.

Acceptance criteria: Tests import title/tune/composer/poet/key/time, named voices, comment-labeled voices, and ignored layout/paper blocks with info diagnostics.

title: LilyPond import: parse pitches, relative octave resolution, durations, ties, rests, chords, and bar checks
description:
What: Implement the supported LilyPond music-event subset: absolute pitches, deterministic relative blocks, note durations 1-64 with dots, ties, rests, chords, simple simultaneous voices, and bar checks.

Why: Melody preservation and bar validation require exact pitch/rhythm import rather than a textual summary.

How: Convert LilyPond pitch spelling and octave marks to normalized pitch strings while preserving source tokens. Convert durations to rational quarter units. Represent simultaneous material as separate voices/events with shared measure IDs/onsets. Treat bar checks as validation aids.

Considerations: Tuplets, grace notes, polymeter, and hidden content-changing macros are unsupported in v1 and should warn/error. Avoid guessing octaves when relative resolution is ambiguous.

Acceptance criteria: Unit tests cover opening hymn phrases, chords, rests, dotted rhythms, ties across measures, relative octave edge cases, simultaneous SATB snippets, and barcheck mismatch diagnostics.

title: LilyPond import: parse repeat volta, alternatives, addlyrics, and lyricmode stanzas
description:
What: Implement restricted LilyPond repeat and lyric extraction for explicit repeat volta bodies, alternative endings, addlyrics blocks, and named lyricmode stanzas.

Why: The trace-shaped hymn source used repeat volta 5 and five addlyrics blocks. Losing those features forces the user to re-explain form and words.

How: Map repeats/alternatives to MusicalStructure sections referencing measure IDs. Create LyricStanza objects, split syllables/hyphenation/extenders where supported, align syllables to lead voice note event IDs when possible, and preserve unaligned stanza text with diagnostics.

Considerations: Repeats whose bodies are macros without literal pasted content are unsupported. Lyric alignment may be incomplete; do not invent words or anchors.

Acceptance criteria: Regression fixture imports five stanzas and a fivefold volta; alternatives reference valid measure IDs; incomplete alignment produces warnings while preserving text.

title: LilyPond import: emit explicit diagnostics for unsupported v1 constructs
description:
What: Build comprehensive diagnostics for unsupported LilyPond constructs in the v1 subset, including Scheme, includes, transpose macros, custom music functions, polymetric staves, tuplets, grace notes, figured bass, and opaque macros.

Why: Silent guessing corrupts imported music and undermines trust. The tool should tell the agent/user why an import is partial and what source conversion would help.

How: Add detection rules with stable diagnostic codes, severity choices, source locations, and suggested next actions such as exporting MusicXML or simplifying the pasted snippet.

Considerations: Some engraving-only overrides can be ignored with info diagnostics; content-affecting unsupported constructs should be warnings/errors depending on impact.

Acceptance criteria: Unit tests assert each unsupported construct returns a specific diagnostic code and does not silently produce misleading MusicDocument events.

title: Extend engrave for multi-staff voices, lyrics, repeats, and validation
description:
What: Extend structured engrave beyond flat tab bars plus optional simple melody so imported MusicDocument arrangements can emit named staves, voices, lyrics, chord symbols/directions, repeats/alternatives, voice + tab layouts, and alfabeto events in multi-staff contexts.

Why: The generic import workflow cannot faithfully preserve hymn/chorale, lead-sheet, or multi-part source material if codegen only supports a narrow tab-centric shape or requires raw LilyPond hand-writing for lyrics/repeats.

How: Add score-level schemas with named parts/voices, stable IDs, lyric attachment to voices/event IDs, section structures referencing measure IDs, chord-symbol/direction placement, rational bar-duration validation per voice, and codegen for lead staff + tab accompaniment.

Considerations: Until this lands, the product must advertise only the narrower melody + simple tab MVP. Validation must run before LilyPond codegen so compile retries are not wasted on known bar-duration, lyric-attachment, repeat, or tab-position errors.

Acceptance criteria: Supported multi-staff engrave inputs compile without raw LilyPond repairs; repeats/alternatives are structurally valid; lyrics attach to named voices; bar durations are checked rationally; alfabeto and explicit tab positions validate before codegen.

title: Engrave: add score-level schemas for named staves, parts, voices, and measure references
description:
What: Extend engrave input schemas to represent a complete score with named staves/parts/voices, stable IDs, measure references, staff groups, and mixed notation/tab contexts.

Why: Imported arrangements need more than a flat tab bar list, especially when preserving a lead staff plus target-instrument accompaniment.

How: Add backward-compatible schema paths or a new versioned shape. Reuse rational duration and measure IDs from the import IR where practical. Validate references before codegen.

Considerations: Avoid breaking existing simple engrave callers. Provide migration/tests for current melody/tab behavior.

Acceptance criteria: Existing engrave tests continue passing; new schemas validate multi-staff voice+tab inputs and reject duplicate IDs, missing measure references, and invalid staff/voice links.

title: Engrave: support lyrics attached to named voices or event IDs
description:
What: Add structured lyric engraving for LyricStanza data attached to named voices or note event IDs.

Why: Hymnary output must preserve words without raw LilyPond lyric hacks.

How: Generate LilyPond lyric variables/contexts tied to the selected lead voice, handling multiple stanzas, hyphenation, extenders where supported, and warnings for unaligned text.

Considerations: Do not invent lyrics or silently drop unaligned stanzas. If alignment is incomplete, engrave stanza 1 or text blocks according to plan diagnostics.

Acceptance criteria: Compile tests cover one stanza, multiple stanzas, hyphenation/extenders, unaligned stanza warnings, and lyric attachment to the correct lead voice.

title: Engrave: support repeat and alternative sections referencing measure IDs
description:
What: Add structured codegen and validation for repeat volta sections and alternative endings that reference imported measure IDs.

Why: Hymn and chorale sources commonly encode repeated stanzas and endings; preserving them is part of source identity.

How: Validate section trees, repeat counts, alternative bodies, and measure references. Emit LilyPond repeat/alternative structures from the normalized section model.

Considerations: Repeats must interact correctly with lyrics and bar-duration validation. Unsupported repeat styles should diagnostic rather than generate invalid LilyPond.

Acceptance criteria: Compile tests cover simple repeats, fivefold volta, alternatives, invalid missing measure IDs, and barcheck correctness through repeated sections.

title: Engrave: decouple lead staff and tab accompaniment bar arrays
description:
What: Support voice + tab layouts where lead material and accompaniment share musical time but are not forced into identical event arrays or compromises that drop melody notes.

Why: For hymns, the lead line may remain in a vocal/notation staff while baroque guitar or lute accompaniment supplies idiomatic material underneath.

How: Model each part/voice independently with measure IDs/onsets/durations. Synchronize by global measures and validate each voice separately. Generate notation staff plus TabStaff/tablature staff in one score.

Considerations: Guitar inability to carry the tune should not justify changing the lead melody. Keep accompaniment idiomatic but subordinate to preservation targets.

Acceptance criteria: Tests compile a lead melody staff plus 5-course baroque guitar accompaniment with differing event densities and no dropped opening phrase identity.

title: Engrave: support alfabeto chord events inside multi-staff score contexts
description:
What: Ensure alfabeto_chord or equivalent resolved alfabeto events work inside the extended multi-staff engrave model, including validation and codegen to explicit tab/course-fret positions.

Why: The hymnary workflow relies on alfabeto for baroque guitar structural strums while preserving a separate lead staff.

How: Reuse the alfabeto_chord event work where available. Validate baroque-guitar-5 instrument compatibility, resolved positions, durations, ties, and bar placement before codegen.

Considerations: Avoid re-calling lookup during codegen if validation already resolved positions. Surface chart/source choices in diagnostics when useful.

Acceptance criteria: Multi-staff compile tests include voice + tab with alfabeto events, mixed melodic tab events in the same bar, and invalid-instrument diagnostics.

title: Derive harmony, preservation targets, and alfabeto arrangement plans from MusicDocument
description:
What: Implement deterministic post-import planning that identifies preservation targets, derives or refines harmony, chooses transposition policy, reduces harmony for target instruments, maps structural baroque guitar chords through alfabeto_lookup, and builds structured engrave input.

Why: Arrangements should preserve profile-required source identity instead of simplifying or displacing the melody to fit an instrument. Historical accompaniment should use source harmony when present and validated playable positions rather than ad-hoc LilyPond edits.

How: Use imported normalized events to derive chord spans/Roman numerals from chord symbols first, then SATB/four-part verticalities, arbitrary multi-part verticalities, or generated conservative harmonization. Infer lead/preservation targets from labels, profiles, texture, register, and user request. Default transpose_policy is ask unless the user requested an instrument-friendly key.

Considerations: Avoid an opaque arrange_music black box. If orchestration exists, it must return intermediate diagnostics. Do not force SATB assumptions onto non-SATB scores. Preserve lead staff when guitar cannot carry the tune faithfully.

Acceptance criteria: Hymn/chorale plans preserve soprano/lead melody, lyrics, repeats, and useful source harmony; lead-sheet plans preserve melody plus chord symbols; melody-only plans state missing lyrics/harmony and proceed conservatively; baroque guitar plans use alfabeto_lookup for structural strums and validate playability before engrave.

title: Arrangement planning: infer preservation targets and ask targeted ambiguity questions
description:
What: Implement preservation-target selection for different profiles: hymn/chorale lead melody and lyrics, lead-sheet melody plus chord symbols, part-song/instrumental named parts, continuo bass/chords, tablature course/fret data, or melody-only lead line.

Why: The arranger should know what material is authoritative before reducing or embellishing anything. This prevents the source melody from being displaced by instrumental idiom.

How: Combine explicit labels, profile candidates, texture analysis, register ordering, source role metadata, expected_content, and user request. If ambiguity materially affects the plan, produce exactly one focused question with candidates and evidence.

Considerations: Single-staff melody sources should preserve the only pitched line. Non-SATB multi-part sources should not be forced into SATB roles. Free paraphrase/reduction is allowed only when requested.

Acceptance criteria: Tests cover soprano default for hymns, explicit lead labels, two plausible upper voices requiring a question, lead-sheet melody+chords, multi-part preservation, and melody-only conservative behavior.

title: Arrangement planning: implement target-instrument transposition policy with evidence
description:
What: Implement plan_transposition for MusicDocument plus target instrument/style, recommending preserve_key, instrument_friendly, or ask with range and idiom evidence.

Why: Baroque guitar and lute arrangements may be more idiomatic in certain keys, but transposition changes the user's source. The system must be explicit rather than silently moving material.

How: Default to ask unless the user already requested an instrument-friendly arrangement or supplied a target key. For 5-course baroque guitar, recognize alfabeto-friendly keys/chords and melody range constraints. Preserve contour and phrase identity if transposing.

Considerations: Do not choose G/C/D/A minor merely because it is easy if the user asked to preserve original key. State original and proposed keys in summaries.

Acceptance criteria: Tests cover Eb-to-G baroque guitar recommendation, preserve_key policy, explicit target key, melody range constraints, and prompt-facing wording for the targeted question.

title: Arrangement planning: derive chord spans and Roman numerals from normalized imported events
description:
What: Implement derive_harmony over MusicDocument/NormalizedAnalysis, returning HarmonyAnalysis with chord spans, pitch classes, Roman numerals where feasible, source tags, and confidence.

Why: Historical accompaniment should reduce source harmony from the imported event graph, not from lossy summaries or model guesses.

How: Prefer explicit chord symbols, then figured bass when supported, SATB/four-part verticality reduction, arbitrary multi-part verticality reduction, and conservative generated harmonization as fallback when no harmony exists.

Considerations: Generated harmony should be clearly marked and conservative for hymn/folk defaults. Roman numeral analysis depends on key confidence and should degrade gracefully.

Acceptance criteria: Tests cover chord-symbol lead sheet, SATB hymn, non-SATB verticalities, no-harmony melody fallback, key ambiguity diagnostics, and stable chord-span durations/onsets.

title: Arrangement planning: reduce harmony for target instruments and map baroque guitar chords to alfabeto
description:
What: Convert HarmonyAnalysis into instrument-appropriate chord/reduction candidates and use alfabeto_lookup for 5-course baroque guitar structural strums.

Why: The baroque guitar workflow should use historically correct alfabeto shapes and validated course/fret data instead of computed or raw LilyPond chord approximations.

How: For baroque-guitar-5, map downbeat/structural chord spans to alfabeto letters/positions, respecting chart_id, max_fret, barre preferences, and source-vs-instrument-friendly reduction diagnostics. For lute-family instruments, leave hooks for voicings/tabulate/diapasons planning.

Considerations: Exact source harmony may need simplification for idiom. If a chord is unavailable or only high-barre, report fallback/diagnostic and decide whether to arpeggiate, omit, transpose, or ask.

Acceptance criteria: Tests include G/C/D/Am/Em progressions, C# minor barred fallback, Foscarini chart selection, source harmony vs reduced harmony diagnostics, and invalid/unavailable chord handling.

title: Arrangement planning: build structured engrave input from MusicDocument and plan
description:
What: Implement build_engrave_input or equivalent deterministic stage that converts selected preservation targets, harmony/reduction plan, transposition plan, and target-instrument idiom into structured engrave input.

Why: Final output should use structured validation/codegen instead of raw LilyPond hand-writing whenever supported features exist.

How: Emit lead/vocal staff, lyrics, repeats, chord symbols/directions, tab staff events, alfabeto events, melodic fills, and layout metadata according to the plan. Return diagnostics and unsupported-feature fallbacks.

Considerations: If engrave lacks multi-staff/repeat support, the builder must produce a scoped melody + simple tab MVP or clearly report unsupported features rather than pretending full v1 completion.

Acceptance criteria: Integration tests turn imported MusicDocument fixtures into valid engrave input; validation catches missing tab positions, bad bar durations, lyric attachment errors, and unsupported repeat structures before compile.

title: Design post-v1 artifact import path for PDF/image/.mscz/MIDI and uncertainty review
description:
What: Design the later music_import_artifact workflow for binary/source artifacts such as PDF, images, MuseScore .mscz, MIDI, and other non-pasted-text inputs.

Why: Users will provide scans and project files, but v1 text import should not pretend to parse binary artifacts without storage, MIME metadata, provenance, conversion backends, or uncertainty review.

How: Specify artifact_id, filename, MIME type, source format, provenance, license, derived MusicXML references, backend versions/commands, confidence metadata, and review UX. Candidate optional backends: MuseScore CLI for .mscz to MusicXML, Audiveris for OMR, Tesseract for lyrics OCR, and Verovio for MEI/ABC rendering/conversion validation.

Considerations: OMR/OCR must expose uncertainty instead of silently guessing. Lyrics OCR is not authoritative musical structure. No durable import persistence is needed in v1 unless the user explicitly saves a source/import.

Acceptance criteria: A future implementer can build music_import_artifact from the issue without re-reading the design doc; v1 docs and prompts clearly ask users to export/convert artifacts to MusicXML or another supported text source.

title: Artifact import: specify music_import_artifact schema, session model, and provenance fields
description:
What: Specify the post-v1 music_import_artifact API for artifact_id, filename, mime_type, source_format, provenance, license, and derived source references.

Why: Direct artifact import needs metadata and storage not present in the v1 pasted-text tool.

How: Define schemas and session-local import behavior. Preserve original artifact references and derived MusicXML SourceArtifactRef entries with backend/version provenance.

Considerations: Do not add durable persistence unless a later saved-import feature requires it. Artifact review should use diagnostics/confidence rather than hiding uncertainty.

Acceptance criteria: Design notes are sufficient to implement the tool later and v1 code paths clearly reject/redirect binary uploads.

title: Artifact import: evaluate optional MuseScore, Audiveris, Tesseract, and Verovio backends
description:
What: Evaluate and document optional server backends for artifact conversion: MuseScore CLI for .mscz to MusicXML, Audiveris for PDF/image OMR, Tesseract for lyrics OCR, and Verovio for MEI/ABC rendering/conversion validation.

Why: Artifact import should be backend-assisted and transparent about runtime requirements, licenses, versions, confidence, and failures.

How: Record install/runtime expectations, command shapes, output formats, license implications, and how backend confidence/diagnostics map into MusicDocument sourceDiagnostics.

Considerations: AGPL/GPL backends are acceptable for this personal project, but they should remain optional/heavy. OCR lyrics are advisory, not authoritative.

Acceptance criteria: Future implementers can choose which backend to wire first and know exactly what metadata/diagnostics to preserve.

title: Artifact import: design uncertainty review UX before arranging uncertain OMR/OCR results
description:
What: Design the post-v1 review flow for uncertain notes, lyrics, repeats, and profile/preservation choices after artifact conversion.

Why: OMR/OCR errors can musically corrupt arrangements if accepted silently. Users need targeted review rather than a blind import.

How: Show source summary, profile candidates, preservation-target choices, diagnostic groups, confidence by note/region where available, and focused questions. Allow proceeding with melody-only output when lyric alignment is incomplete.

Considerations: V1 uses structured diagnostics plus agent questions instead of a rich side panel. This issue is for the later UI.

Acceptance criteria: UX design covers PDF/image, .mscz-derived MusicXML, uncertain lyrics, ambiguous repeats, and low-confidence note regions.

title: Music import: expose server-backed music_import tool with inline MusicDocument result
description:
What: Add the music_import tool to the MCP/tool surface with source, source_format, source_name, mime_type, provenance, license, expected_profile, and expected_content parameters, returning a summary plus full structured MusicDocument inline.

Why: Agents need a deterministic import stage before arrangement. Inline results are simpler for v1 pasted text and avoid introducing session persistence prematurely.

How: Implement external snake_case schemas and map to internal camelCase. Route source_format=auto through detection; dispatch to MusicXML, restricted LilyPond, ABC/MEI/plain-text stubs or diagnostics as supported. Include ranked detections, diagnostics, confidence, suggested next actions, and canonical document in structured data.

Considerations: Do not accept binary PDF/image/.mscz/MIDI uploads in this v1 tool. Do not name it hymn_import. Large artifact workflows may add transient import IDs later if inline payloads become too large.

Acceptance criteria: Tool is registered, typechecked, documented in prompt, and tested for MusicXML, LilyPond, plain lyrics, unsupported binary-format hints, expected_profile overrides, and expected_content diagnostics.

title: Music import: update system prompt and tests for import-normalize-arrange policy
description:
What: Update src/prompts.ts and tests so the agent uses music_import before arranging from pasted notation/lyrics/text sources and treats import, normalization, planning, engrave, and compile as separate stages.

Why: The trace failure was largely policy: the agent improvised a solo guitar paraphrase before preserving the user's source identity. Prompt policy should make the desired workflow the default.

How: Add a concise import workflow section. Instruct the agent to preserve profile-required material; for hymn/chorale sources preserve lead melody, lyrics, repeats, and SATB/four-part harmony if present; use alfabeto_lookup for baroque guitar; ask targeted questions only for ambiguity; request/export MusicXML for v1 unsupported artifacts.

Considerations: Prompt should not imply full PDF/image/.mscz import or full generic LilyPond support. It should state fallback when engrave lacks multi-staff/repeat features.

Acceptance criteria: Prompt tests assert presence of music_import, MusicDocument/import-normalize-arrange language, hymn/chorale lead preservation, artifact-to-MusicXML guidance, and alfabeto_lookup/baroque guitar workflow language.

title: Music import: document v1 scope boundaries, supported source subset, and user-facing diagnostics
description:
What: Add or update project documentation describing v1 text import capabilities, non-goals, restricted LilyPond subset, preferred MusicXML workflow, and fallback behavior when engrave features are not yet available.

Why: The system must avoid over-promising universal notation import, OMR accuracy, direct binary parsing, or copyright decisions. Clear scope also guides agent responses.

How: Document supported source formats, explicit unsupported constructs, diagnostic meanings, expected user flows, and examples of targeted questions. Reference HYMNARY-IMPORT-SPEC.md design decisions without requiring users to read it.

Considerations: Keep wording generic: hymn/chorale is a high-quality profile, not the only use case. State that source rights are preserved/displayed but user responsibility remains.

Acceptance criteria: Docs explain MusicXML-first import, restricted LilyPond support, ABC/MEI/plain text status, artifact limitations, and the melody + simple tab MVP fallback if multi-staff engrave is pending.

title: Fix compile include path for engrave-generated sources
description: Engrave emits includes like instruments/baroque-lute-13.ily, but compile-route only passed instruments/ and templates/ as LilyPond include dirs. Compiling engrave output could not find the instrument .ily file. Add project root to LilyPond include dirs and test it.

title: Refactor: move parseTimeSignature to shared lib
description:
## What
parseTimeSignature is a private utility in engrave.ts that will be needed by other tools.

## Fix
Move to src/lib/music-utils.ts (new file) and export. Update engrave.ts to import from there.

## Acceptance criteria
- parseTimeSignature exported from shared module
- engrave.ts imports from shared location
- Unit tests for the function

title: Fix: LyFile.variables unconditionally wraps values in braces
description:
## What
Change `LyFile.variables` serialization in `src/lib/ly-tree.ts` so callers can control whether values are wrapped in `{ }` braces.

## Why
The current serializer unconditionally wraps variable values: `${name} = { ${body} }`. For music expressions this is correct, but for mode-specific content like `\lyricmode { Sing now... }`, this produces `lyricsText = { \lyricmode { ... } }` — unnecessary double-wrapping.

The voice-and-tab template needs `lyricsText = \lyricmode { ... }` (no outer braces). Other use cases may need bare assignments like `myTuning = \stringTuning <...>`.

## How
Change `variables` from `Record<string, string>` to `Array<{ name: string; body: string; braces?: boolean }>`:
- `braces: true` (default) → `name = { body }`
- `braces: false` → `name = body`

Alternative: keep `Record<string, string>` but don't add braces — let callers include `{ }` in the value when needed. This is simpler but requires callers to know the convention.

## Acceptance criteria
- Variables can be emitted with or without wrapping braces
- `\lyricmode { ... }` can be used as a variable value without double-wrapping
- Existing tests updated for any signature changes
- New test for brace-free variable emission
close_reason: done
